theorem ker_range_invtSubmodule_of_commute
    {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]
    (f g : Module.End K V)
    (hfg : f * g = g * f) :

node.natural_language_proof:
Proof: assume `f g = g f`. For the kernel, let `x ∈ ker f`, so `f x = 0`. Then
`f (g x) = (f g) x = (g f) x = g (f x) = 0`, hence `g x ∈ ker f`.
For the range, take `y ∈ range f`, so `y = f x` for some `x`. Then
`g y = g (f x) = (g f) x = (f g) x = f (g x)`, which lies again in `range f`.

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LinearMap.End
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Ker
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Range
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Invariant

variable {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]

theorem ker_range_invtSubmodule_of_commute
    (f g : Module.End K V)
    (hfg : f * g = g * f) :
    LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule ∧ LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule := by
  constructor
  · -- Prove LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    -- This means g maps ker f to itself, i.e., ∀ x ∈ ker f, g x ∈ ker f
    apply Submodule.mem_invtSubmodule.2
    intro x hx
    have : f (g x) = 0 := by
      calc
        f (g x) = (f * g) x := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
        _ = (g * f) x := by rw [hfg]
        _ = g (f x) := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
        _ = g 0 := by rw [hx]
        _ = 0 := by simp
    exact this
  · -- Prove LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    -- This means g maps range f to itself, i.e., ∀ y ∈ range f, g y ∈ range f
    apply Submodule.mem_invtSubmodule.2
    intro y hy
    rcases hy with ⟨x, rfl⟩
    use g x
    rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
    rw [hfg]
    rw [Module.End.mul_apply]

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LinearMap.End
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Ker
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Range
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Invariant

variable {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]

theorem ker_range_invtSubmodule_of_commute
    (f g : Module.End K V)
    (hfg : f * g = g * f) :
    LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule ∧ LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule := by
  constructor
  · -- Prove LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    -- This means ker f is invariant under g, i.e., g(ker f) ⊆ ker f
    have h₁ : LinearMap.ker f.carrier ⊆ g ⁻¹' LinearMap.ker f.carrier := by
      intro x hx
      -- x ∈ ker f means f x = 0
      -- We need to show f (g x) = 0
      have h₂ : f (g x) = 0 := by
        calc
          f (g x) = (f * g) x := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = (g * f) x := by rw [hfg]
          _ = g (f x) := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = g 0 := by rw [hx]
          _ = 0 := by simp
      exact h₂
    exact Set.mem_of_subset h₁
  · -- Prove LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    have h₂ : g '' LinearMap.range f.carrier ⊆ LinearMap.range f.carrier := by
      intro y hy
      rcases hy with ⟨x, rfl⟩
      use g x
      rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
      rw [hfg]
      rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
    exact Set.mem_of_subset h₂

import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.LinearMap.End
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Ker
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Range
import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.Submodule.Invariant

variable {K V : Type*} [CommSemiring K] [AddCommMonoid V] [Module K V]

theorem ker_range_invtSubmodule_of_commute
    (f g : Module.End K V)
    (hfg : f * g = g * f) :
    LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule ∧ LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule := by
  constructor
  · -- Prove LinearMap.ker f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    -- This means ker f ≤ (ker f).comap g
    have h₁ : LinearMap.ker f ≤ LinearMap.ker f.comap g := by
      intro x hx
      have h₂ : f (g x) = 0 := by
        calc
          f (g x) = (f * g) x := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = (g * f) x := by rw [hfg]
          _ = g (f x) := by rw [Module.End.mul_apply]
          _ = g 0 := by rw [hx]
          _ = 0 := by simp
      exact h₂
    exact h₁
  · -- Prove LinearMap.range f ∈ g.invtSubmodule
    have h₂ : LinearMap.range f ≤ LinearMap.range f.comap g := by
      intro y hy
      rcases hy with ⟨x, rfl⟩
      use g x
      rw [Module.End.mul_apply]

    exact h₂

declaration.natural_language: Given a semilinear map \(f : M \to M_2\) and a submodule \(p \subseteq M_2\), the construction \(\mathrm{comap}(f,p)\) is the submodule of \(M\) consisting of all vectors \(x \in M\) whose image under \(f\) lies in \(p\), i.e. \(\{x \in M \mid f(x)\in p\}\), with the induced \(R\)-module structure. In other words, it is the pullback of \(p\) along \(f\), and it is the largest \(R\)-submodule of \(M\) mapping into \(p\) under \(f\).
